transcript: I often dream of the birds I've met in Hawaii, the mighty albatross, Newell's Shearwaters, strange little Gonzo. However, when I reminisce my thoughts will circle back to a small black bird, the Kauai O'o. By the time I got to the islands, they were practically extinct. But deep in the Alakai'i, I found them. A pair. On the outside, they're quite ordinary. But here, listen to them sing. One will go. And the other. That was the first time I saw them. As for the last time. Hmm. Do you think it knows? If it's the last one... I don't know. What matters is that it's still alive. And we've captured its call. It'll live on. Wanna listen? Sure. What is it doing? Jacoby, wait. What is it doing? Jacoby, wait. Play the recording again. What? Just play it again. Thank you. I can't tell you what happened next. Soon after, I returned to pennsylvania but i thought about it often if it went back to its nest if it kept looking if it kept singing more than anything i hope it passed away softly peacefully in the middle of a dream.
